Explanation: The BASE_URL column uses an ENUM type (BaseUrlType) to restrict allowed values, which makes adding or removing valid values difficult and requires schema changes. Consider creating a separate lookup table for base URL types and referencing it with a foreign key.

Using selectFrom(CHATS) fetches all columns from the CHATS table, which may retrieve unnecessary data. Consider explicitly selecting only the required columns using select(CHATS.CHAT_ID, CHATS.CREATED_AT).from(CHATS).
Using selectFrom(CHATS) fetches all columns from the CHATS table into a ChatsRecord. Consider explicitly selecting only the columns needed using select(...).from(CHATS).

Using select() without specifying columns fetches all columns from the joined tables, which may include unnecessary data. Consider explicitly listing the required columns like select(CHATS.CHAT_ID, CHATS.CREATED_AT).
Using selectFrom(LINKS) fetches all columns from the LINKS table, which may retrieve unnecessary data and impact performance. Consider using an explicit select() with only the required columns.
Using selectFrom(LINKS) fetches all columns from the LINKS table into a LinksRecord. Consider explicitly selecting only the columns needed for the Link object.
Using select() without specifying columns fetches all columns from the joined tables. Consider explicitly listing only the required columns from LINKS.
Using selectFrom(LINKS) fetches all columns from the LINKS table. Consider using an explicit select() with only the columns needed for the Link mapping.
